title: atractocarpus oblongus
intro: Atractocarpus oblongus, commonly known as the 'Red Bead Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Eurytomaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially lowering blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
